quick_facts.label: Role focus
quick_facts.value: A Senior Corporate Counsel is a senior in-house legal adviser who independently supports business programmes and commercial relationships, including complex contracts, regulatory/compliance issues, and pre-litigation risk.

quick_facts.label: Core legal qualification
quick_facts.value: The usual educational foundation is an LL.B. from a law programme recognised for advocate-enrolment purposes by the Bar Council of India; employers may specify an Indian LL.B. or an equivalent common-law degree for particular roles.

quick_facts.label: Practice credential context
quick_facts.value: State-Bar enrolment and the All India Bar Examination are the regulatory route for lawyers seeking a certificate of practice and court practice; candidates should assess this separately from an employer’s requirements for an in-house role.

quick_facts.label: Experience context
quick_facts.value: This is normally an experienced role rather than an entry-level designation. Illustrative India postings have requested 10+ or 13+ years of relevant corporate/legal advisory experience, but the threshold is employer- and sector-specific.

sections.heading: What a Senior Corporate Counsel does
paragraphs: The role is principally a business-facing legal leadership position. Depending on the company and sector, the counsel may structure, draft, review and negotiate high-value commercial arrangements; advise on new products, transactions and operating models; translate legal requirements into practical business advice; and resolve issues in existing commercial relationships or pre-litigation disputes.

In India-facing technology and marketplace work, the legal portfolio can span foreign-investment, competition, consumer-protection, privacy, IT and intellectual-property questions. In regulated or multinational organisations, the role can also involve monitoring legal developments, advising on regulatory interactions, supporting compliance programmes and managing external counsel.

Corporate-governance work may be part of the remit or carried out closely with company secretarial colleagues. The Companies Act, 2013 includes requirements concerning company registers and registers of contracts or arrangements in which directors are interested, so scope should be clarified carefully in the employer’s job description.
bullets: Lead or materially shape complex contract negotiations and risk allocation.
Advise senior business stakeholders on legally sound, commercially workable options.
Identify material legal and regulatory risk, recommend mitigations, and escalate issues requiring leadership decisions.
Coordinate with finance, tax, compliance, privacy, policy, security, operations and external counsel.
Support governance, compliance, disputes and strategic projects where these sit within the legal function.

sections.heading: Education and route into senior in-house work
paragraphs: A common starting point is a three-year or five-year law degree from an institution whose law degree is recognised by the Bar Council of India for enrolment purposes. The Bar Council of India states that it sets standards for legal education and recognises universities whose law degree serves as a qualification for enrolment as an advocate.

For candidates who intend to practise before courts, the regulatory route includes enrolment with a State Bar Council and the AIBE certificate-of-practice process. The Bar Council of India describes AIBE as a post-enrolment examination for law graduates seeking to practise the profession; this court-practice pathway should not be confused with the separate hiring criteria set by each in-house employer.

Career progression typically requires several years of substantive responsibility in a law firm, an in-house legal team, or both. As an illustrative employer pathway, Amazon’s India Associate Corporate Counsel posting sought 3–5 years of post-qualification experience and stated that the associate would report to a Corporate Counsel or Senior Corporate Counsel; its Senior Corporate Counsel posting sought extensive corporate-law experience. This supports, but does not guarantee, progression from junior/associate counsel to corporate counsel and then senior counsel.
bullets: Choose a BCI-recognised/approved legal-education route if advocate enrolment is a career objective.
Build practical capability through internships and early roles involving commercial contracts, corporate advisory, regulatory work, transactions or disputes.
Develop ownership of matters: drafting, negotiations, client advice, risk assessment and coordination with business teams.
Move into broader, more autonomous in-house or law-firm advisory work before targeting senior counsel positions.

sections.heading: Skills employers expect
paragraphs: Strong legal analysis, contract drafting and negotiation are central. Senior counsel must turn complex legal and factual material into clear advice, assess the business impact of risk, and design practical solutions rather than merely identify issues.

The role also demands commercial judgement and stakeholder leadership. Current India role descriptions emphasise independent work with legal and business partners, prioritisation of multiple fast-moving matters, clear written and verbal communication, organisational discipline, and the ability to coordinate diverse internal and external stakeholders.

The technical mix changes with the business. For example, digital and marketplace teams may require capability across privacy, consumer protection, competition, intellectual property and technology regulation. India’s Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 provides the statutory context for one major area that may arise in data-driven businesses.
bullets: Commercial-contract drafting, review and negotiation.
Legal research, issue spotting, risk assessment and concise advisory writing.
Business judgement: balancing legal risk, commercial goals and escalation needs.
Influencing and communicating with executives and cross-functional teams.
Project management, prioritisation and external-counsel management.
Relevant sector knowledge, such as technology, data, financial services, manufacturing, healthcare or regulated commerce.

sections.heading: Work setting and stakeholders
paragraphs: Senior Corporate Counsel usually works inside a company legal department as a business partner, often reporting to a senior legal leader such as an Associate General Counsel, Director-Legal or General Counsel. The position may be an individual-contributor role even at senior level, while other employers combine the role with leadership and mentoring responsibilities.

The work is cross-functional: senior counsel routinely partners with business, sales, product, operations, finance, tax, compliance, privacy, security, public-policy and litigation teams. Multinational organisations may also require coordination with regional or global legal teams and external advisers across jurisdictions.

Location, travel, working hours and remote-work arrangements are employer-specific. For example, an Amazon India posting described a Bengaluru-based role with possible domestic and international travel, while a separate India listing described support for both India business and cross-border APAC projects.
bullets: In-house legal department, embedded in commercial and operational decision-making.
Frequent collaboration with senior internal stakeholders and specialist functions.
Regular interface with external counsel, regulators or government authorities where matters require it.
Possible regional or global scope in multinational companies.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: A typical progression is from legal intern or junior lawyer to associate/assistant counsel, Corporate Counsel or Legal Counsel, then Senior Corporate Counsel/Senior Legal Counsel. From there, professionals may move into specialist leadership—such as commercial, compliance, privacy, regulatory or product legal leadership—or broader management roles including Associate General Counsel, General Counsel or Head of Legal.

Progression depends more on increasing autonomy, complexity, business trust and leadership than on title alone. Evidence of readiness includes leading complex negotiations, advising senior decision-makers, managing major risk or regulatory matters, improving legal processes, and mentoring or leading colleagues.

Titles are not standardised across employers. An Amazon India Associate Corporate Counsel posting explicitly places the associate below Corporate Counsel/Senior Corporate Counsel, while a Salesforce Senior Corporate Counsel posting includes leadership and mentoring of legal professionals. Candidates should therefore compare reporting line, decision authority and matter complexity—not only designation.
bullets: Early career: internships, junior legal roles, law-firm associate work or associate counsel roles.
Mid-career: independently manage contract portfolios, advisory matters and defined business clients.
Senior level: lead material transactions or programmes, influence senior leaders, and own major legal-risk areas.
Later options: specialist legal lead, Associate General Counsel, General Counsel, Head of Legal or legal-operations leadership.
